insecure_services_linux.name: Telnet (TCP 23)
insecure_services_linux.notes: Sends credentials and session data in plaintext; easily captured via sniffing.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Disable service. Replace with SSH (TCP 22) using strong ciphers and key-based authentication.

insecure_services_linux.name: rlogin / rsh / rexec
insecure_services_linux.notes: No encryption; relies on weak trust-based authentication between hosts.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Remove entirely. Use SSH with centralized authentication (e.g., LDAP, Kerberos).

insecure_services_linux.name: FTP (TCP 21)
insecure_services_linux.notes: Username, password, and file data transmitted in cleartext.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Replace with SFTP (SSH-based) or FTPS (TLS). If not removable, restrict via firewall and encrypt tunnel (VPN).

insecure_services_linux.name: TFTP (UDP 69)
insecure_services_linux.notes: No authentication or encryption; allows unauthorized file access/transfer.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Disable if unused. If required (e.g., network boot), restrict to isolated network/VLAN and ACLs.

insecure_services_linux.name: HTTP (TCP 80)
insecure_services_linux.notes: Data transmitted without encryption; susceptible to interception/manipulation.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Enforce HTTPS (TLS 1.2+), redirect HTTP to HTTPS, disable weak ciphers (SSL, TLS 1.0/1.1).

insecure_services_linux.name: SMTP (no TLS)
insecure_services_linux.notes: Email content may be transmitted unencrypted between servers.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Enforce STARTTLS or SMTPS, configure secure mail relay, restrict open relay.

insecure_services_linux.name: POP3 / IMAP (110/143)
insecure_services_linux.notes: Credentials and email data may be exposed in plaintext.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Use POP3S (995) or IMAPS (993) with TLS; disable plaintext ports.

insecure_services_linux.name: SNMP v1 / v2c
insecure_services_linux.notes: Uses plaintext community strings for authentication.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Upgrade to SNMPv3 (auth + encryption); restrict access via ACLs and management VLANs.

insecure_services_linux.name: NFS (insecure configs)
insecure_services_linux.notes: May expose file systems and transmit data unencrypted.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Use NFSv4 with Kerberos, restrict by host/IP, enforce firewall segmentation.

insecure_services_linux.name: SMB (SMBv1/v2)
insecure_services_linux.notes: Older versions vulnerable to exploits (e.g., ransomware); weak/no encryption.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Disable SMBv1/v2; enforce SMBv3 with encryption/signing enabled.

insecure_services_linux.name: finger / rwhod
insecure_services_linux.notes: Exposes user/system information useful for reconnaissance.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Disable service entirely; no secure replacement typically required.

insecure_services_linux.name: authd / identd
insecure_services_linux.notes: Leaks identity/user information; limited security benefit.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Disable unless explicitly required; restrict access if enabled.

insecure_services_linux.name: RPC / rpcbind
insecure_services_linux.notes: Exposes internal services; commonly targeted during enumeration.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Disable if unused; otherwise restrict to internal networks and firewall allowlists.

insecure_services_linux.name: Sendmail (misconfigured)
insecure_services_linux.notes: Can allow open relay or abuse if not hardened.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Restrict to localhost or authenticated relay; consider hardened MTAs (Postfix with TLS).

insecure_services_linux.name: Netdump / remote dump services
insecure_services_linux.notes: Transfers memory dumps (may include sensitive data) over network unencrypted.
insecure_services_linux.remediation: Disable or restrict tightly; if required, isolate and encrypt transport.

insecure_services_windows.name: Telnet (TCP 23)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Transmits credentials in plaintext; deprecated and easily intercepted.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Disable Windows Telnet Server feature. Replace with RDP (secured) or SSH (OpenSSH on Windows).

insecure_services_windows.name: FTP (TCP 21)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Sends credentials and data unencrypted; susceptible to sniffing and manipulation.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Replace with SFTP (SSH) or FTPS (TLS); enforce encryption for all file transfers.

insecure_services_windows.name: HTTP (TCP 80)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Data transmitted without encryption; vulnerable to interception and MITM attacks.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Enforce HTTPS (TLS 1.2+), disable plaintext HTTP, and redirect traffic to HTTPS.

insecure_services_windows.name: SMBv1
insecure_services_windows.notes: Deprecated protocol with no encryption, weak authentication, and major exploit history (e.g., WannaCry).
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Disable SMBv1. Use SMBv2/SMBv3 with signing and encryption enabled.

insecure_services_windows.name: SMBv1 / SMBv2 (older configs)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Older SMB versions lack modern protections and are vulnerable to exploitation.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Enforce SMBv3, enable SMB signing, and enable encryption where supported.

insecure_services_windows.name: NetBIOS (ports 137–139)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Legacy name resolution and session protocol commonly abused for enumeration and lateral movement.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Disable NetBIOS over TCP/IP where possible; rely on DNS and modern SMB (port 445).

insecure_services_windows.name: LLMNR (Link-Local Multicast Name Resolution)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Enables credential poisoning and relay attacks; commonly abused in internal networks.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Disable via Group Policy; rely on DNS-only name resolution.

insecure_services_windows.name: NTLM (legacy authentication)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Weak/fallback authentication allows credential relay and reuse attacks.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Restrict or disable NTLM where possible; enforce Kerberos authentication and strong policies.

insecure_services_windows.name: SNMP v1 / v2c
insecure_services_windows.notes: Uses plaintext community strings with no encryption.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Upgrade to SNMPv3 (authentication + encryption); restrict access via ACLs and management VLANs.

insecure_services_windows.name: Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) – weak configs
insecure_services_windows.notes: Weak ciphers or lack of NLA can expose sessions to interception or brute-force attacks.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Enforce NLA, TLS 1.2+, strong ciphers (AES-GCM), MFA, and restrict access via VPN/firewall.

insecure_services_windows.name: LDAP (unencrypted, TCP 389)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Credentials may be transmitted without encryption if not secured.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Use LDAPS (TCP 636) and enforce signing and channel binding.

insecure_services_windows.name: RPC / MS-RPC (port 135)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Exposes internal services and is frequently targeted for enumeration and lateral movement.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Restrict via firewall to trusted hosts; disable unused RPC services where possible.

insecure_services_windows.name: VNC (unencrypted variants)
insecure_services_windows.notes: Weak or no encryption; credentials often exposed.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Replace with secure RDP or tunnel VNC through SSH or VPN with encryption.

insecure_services_windows.name: Remote Registry Service
insecure_services_windows.notes: Allows remote modification of the registry, increasing attack surface.
insecure_services_windows.remediation: Disable service unless explicitly required; restrict access via firewall.
